intro: Graphephorum fisheri, also known as Fisher's grappenh, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful for the treatment of various conditions such as wounds and infections. Bioactive constituents found in Graphephorum fisheri include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Graphephorum fisheri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits.
